title: Agropyron pouzolzii
taxonomy: Agropyron pouzolzii is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Agropyron, which is a group of perennial grasses commonly found in temperate regions. More specifically, Agropyron pouzolzii is classified as a member of the tribe Triticeae, within the subfamily Pooideae. The subfamily Pooideae is a large group of grasses that are often found in grasslands and meadows. This classification suggests that Agropyron pouzolzii shares characteristics with other grasses in this family.

intro: Agropyron pouzolzii, also known as crested wheatgrass or Poa sibirica, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Agropyron pouzolzii include saponins, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Agropyron pouzolzii is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
